pattern: Work backward with two boolean DP arrays — reachable via an odd jump and via an even jump — where each jump's destination is the next-larger-or-equal (or next-smaller-or-equal) index found by sorting with a monotonic stack.
hints: Whether an index is good depends only on where its next jump lands and whether that landing is good for the opposite parity, so think backward from the last index.
For odd jumps you always go to the smallest value that is at least the current one (ties to the nearest index); for even jumps, the largest value that is at most the current one.

Sort indices by value (ascending for odd, descending for even) and use a monotonic stack to precompute each index's odd-jump and even-jump target. Then fill odd[i]/even[i] from the back: the last index is good for both, and i is good (odd) if its odd target is even-good, etc. Count odd-good indices.

edge_inputs.description: all equal values, every jump lands on the nearest equal
tie-breaking picks the smallest index among equal targets

stress.description: random array with a small value range so ties and both jump types fire
stress.note: low cardinality stresses tie-breaking between equal targets
